calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Off Calendar

Application For Entry Of Default Court Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-19-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Gail Dekreon, Clerk: A. Mok. Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Keith H. Rutman, Esq., in Pro Per, appearing for Plaintiffs Keith H. Rutman, Todd D. Rutman, and Seth A. Rutman through telephone, sworn and examined,
No appearance by defaulted defendants TITO CARDONA, MATTHEW JORGENSON, HERMAN WHEATLEY IV, and JENNER VIGIL,
Default of Defendants has been duly entered.
Court ordered Defendants Does 5-20 dismissed with prejudice.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs and against Defaulted Defendants as follows:

Damages: $952,099.61
Pre-Judgment Interest: $0.00
Attorney's Fees: $0.00
Costs: $6019.05
Punitives: $2,000,000.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $2,958,118.65

Judgment signed in open court.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-30-2017 Continued To Jan-30-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Application For Entry Of Default Court Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-30-2017 Continued To Dec-19-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Keith N. Rutman, Esq. appeared on behalf of himself, and on behalf of his brothers, Todd M. Rutman and Seth A. Rutman, who are not present at this morning's hearing but are on telephone standby needed.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Herman Wheatley, IV, Jenner Virgil, Matthew Jorgenson or Tito Cardona.
Defaults were entered against all four defendants on August 18, 2017.
DOES 5 through 20 were dismissed on August 28, 2017.

Good cause appearing, this matter is CONTINUED to December 19, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. for further default prove-up hearing. Plaintiffs shall provide the Court with supplemental documentation in support of their claim for damages.

Plaintiffs may appear via CourtCall at the next calling of the case.
